title: A child in Krakow who corrected my pronunciation
summary: A short story about a child who, without any shyness, fixed how you said a word abroad.
topic: A child in Krakow who corrected my pronunciation
prompt: Write 8–10 sentences (150–250 words) about a child abroad who corrected the way you said a word. Describe where it happened, what they did, and how you felt. Use past simple and past continuous.
hintStarters: I was buying bread at a small shop in…
Behind me, a little girl in a red coat…
I expected her parents to apologise, but…
sampleText: I was buying bread at a small shop near Plac Nowy in Krakow on a Saturday morning. There was a short queue, and I tried to ask for a 'chleb żytni' — rye bread — but I pronounced 'żytni' wrong, with a flat 'z' instead of the soft sound. The woman behind the counter looked confused and asked me to repeat. I tried again. It was still wrong. Behind me, a little girl in a red coat, maybe six years old, tugged on her father's sleeve and whispered something. Then, to my surprise, she stepped forward, looked up at me, and said the word slowly, breaking it into three soft syllables. I repeated after her. She shook her head and said it again. I tried once more. This time she smiled and said, 'Tak.' The woman behind the counter laughed and handed me the bread. The girl's father just shrugged and smiled, as if this happened often. I walked out with the warm loaf under my arm, feeling slightly embarrassed and very grateful. Children correct you because they have not yet learned to pretend they did not notice. Three years later, I still hear that little voice every time I order rye bread.

sampleAnnotations.phrase: I was buying bread at a small shop
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: Tôi đang mua bánh mì ở một tiệm nhỏ

sampleAnnotations.phrase: The woman behind the counter looked confused
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: Người phụ nữ sau quầy có vẻ bối rối

sampleAnnotations.phrase: a little girl in a red coat
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: một cô bé mặc áo khoác đỏ
giật tay áo bố cô bé

sampleAnnotations.phrase: breaking it into three soft syllables
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: tách nó thành ba âm tiết nhẹ
Tôi lặp lại theo cô bé
như thể chuyện này hay xảy ra

sampleAnnotations.phrase: with the warm loaf under my arm
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: với ổ bánh mì ấm dưới cánh tay

sampleAnnotations.phrase: feeling slightly embarrassed and very grateful
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: cảm thấy hơi ngại và rất biết ơn

sampleAnnotations.phrase: I still hear that little voice every time
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: Tôi vẫn nghe giọng nhỏ ấy mỗi lần

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Past continuous for setting a scene
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: 'I was buying bread at a small shop' dùng past continuous để dựng cảnh; sau đó các sự kiện chính ('tugged', 'stepped', 'said') ở past simple xảy ra trong cảnh đó.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: 'I was buying bread at a small shop' uses past continuous to set the scene; then the main events ('tugged', 'stepped', 'said') in past simple happen inside that scene.

sampleGrammarNotes.title: 'As if' + past simple for unreal comparison
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: 'as if this happened often' dùng past simple sau 'as if' để diễn tả sự so sánh — không phải sự kiện có thật, mà là cảm giác về tình huống.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: 'As if this happened often' uses past simple after 'as if' to express a comparison — not a real fact, but a sense of the situation.

sampleTranslationVi: Tôi đang mua bánh mì ở một tiệm nhỏ gần Plac Nowy ở Krakow vào một sáng thứ Bảy. Có một hàng người ngắn, và tôi cố gọi 'chleb żytni' — bánh mì lúa mạch đen — nhưng tôi phát âm 'żytni' sai, với âm 'z' phẳng thay vì âm nhẹ. Người phụ nữ sau quầy có vẻ bối rối và yêu cầu tôi lặp lại. Tôi thử lại. Vẫn sai. Phía sau tôi, một cô bé mặc áo khoác đỏ, có lẽ sáu tuổi, giật tay áo bố cô bé và thì thầm gì đó. Sau đó, làm tôi ngạc nhiên, cô bé bước lên, nhìn lên tôi, và nói từ đó chậm rãi, tách thành ba âm tiết nhẹ. Tôi lặp lại theo cô bé. Cô bé lắc đầu và nói lại lần nữa. Tôi thử thêm một lần. Lần này cô bé mỉm cười và nói, 'Tak.' Người phụ nữ sau quầy bật cười và đưa bánh mì cho tôi. Bố cô bé chỉ nhún vai và mỉm cười, như thể chuyện này hay xảy ra. Tôi bước ra với ổ bánh mì ấm dưới cánh tay, cảm thấy hơi ngại và rất biết ơn. Trẻ con sửa cho bạn vì chúng chưa học cách giả vờ không nhận ra. Ba năm sau, tôi vẫn nghe giọng nhỏ ấy mỗi lần gọi bánh mì lúa mạch đen.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct past continuous opening.
options: I bought bread at a small shop near Plac Nowy.
I am buying bread at a small shop near Plac Nowy.
I was buying bread at a small shop near Plac Nowy.
I have buy bread at a small shop near Plac Nowy.
mcQuestions.explanation: Past continuous 'was buying' sets the scene.

mcQuestions.prompt: Pick the natural past simple action sentence.
options: She step forward and said the word slowly.
She was stepping forward and said the word slowly.
She stepped forward and saying the word slowly.
She stepped forward and said the word slowly.
mcQuestions.explanation: Two past simple verbs for two finished actions.

options: to pull sharply or quickly
to push gently
to throw away
to put down
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Tug' = pull with a short sharp motion.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct preposition: 'tugged ___ her father's sleeve'.
options: in
on
at
to

mcQuestions.prompt: Pick the correct sentence with 'as if'.
options: He shrugged, as if this is happen often.
He shrugged, as if this happens often.
He shrugged, as if this happened often.
He shrugged, as if this has happen often.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'As if + past simple' for an imagined or general comparison.

options: the meaning of a word
the spelling of a word
the writer of a word
the way a word is said
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Pronunciation' = how a word sounds when spoken.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct sentence with 'shake one's head'.
options: She shook her head and said it again.
She shaked her head and said it again.
She shake her head and said it again.
She was shake her head and said it again.

mcQuestions.prompt: Pick the natural sentence with 'repeat after'.
options: I repeated to her.
I repeated after her.
I repeated by her.
I repeated of her.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Repeat after (someone)' = say it the way they just said it.

options: in a hospital
very young
able to wait calmly without getting annoyed
very quiet

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct sentence with two adjectives.
options: I felt slight embarrassed and very grateful.
I feel slightly embarrassed and very grateful.
I felt slightly embarrassing and very grateful.
I felt slightly embarrassed and very grateful.
mcQuestions.explanation: Adverb 'slightly' modifies 'embarrassed' (past participle adjective). Past simple 'felt'.

options: I still hear that little voice every time I order rye bread.
I am still hear that little voice every time I order rye bread.
I still hearing that little voice every time I order rye bread.
I still heard that little voice every time I order rye bread.
mcQuestions.explanation: Present simple 'hear' + 'every time' for a recurring present habit.

criticalThinkingQuestion: Why are children often better language teachers than adults — and why are adults often worse students when children try to teach them?
